title: chroot isolation: RUN steps lack /dev/urandom, breaking OpenSSL 3.x TLS
body:
## Description

When using `buildah build --isolation=chroot` (or `podman build --isolation=chroot`), `RUN` steps do not have access to `/dev/urandom`. This causes OpenSSL 3.x to fail with PRNG seeding errors, breaking any HTTPS operation (curl, wget, etc.) inside a `RUN` instruction.

## Environment

- **Buildah/Podman**: Running inside Kubernetes (Jenkins CI), using `podman build --isolation=chroot`
- **Base image**: `ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner:latest` (Ubuntu 24.04 with OpenSSL 3.x)
- **Host**: Amazon Linux 2023, kernel 6.1.x

## Steps to Reproduce

```dockerfile
FROM ubuntu:24.04
RUN curl -fsSL https://example.com/somefile.tar.gz -o /tmp/file.tar.gz
```

```bash
podman build --isolation=chroot .
```

## Expected Behavior

`curl` (or any TLS client) should work inside `RUN` steps, as it does with `--isolation=oci` and with Docker.

## Actual Behavior

```
curl: (35) Insufficient randomness
```

Or with `wget`:

```
Could not seed PRNG; consider using --random-file.
OpenSSL: error:12800067:DSO support routines::could not load the shared library
OpenSSL: error:0308010C:digital envelope routines::unsupported
OpenSSL: error:12000090:random number generator::unable to fetch drbg
```

## Root Cause

Chroot isolation does not bind-mount `/dev/urandom` (or `/dev/random`) into the chroot for `RUN` steps. Older base images with OpenSSL 1.1.x tolerated this because their PRNG seeding was less strict. OpenSSL 3.x requires proper entropy sources and fails hard when `/dev/urandom` is unavailable.

## Suggested Fix

Bind-mount `/dev/urandom` and `/dev/random` from the host into the chroot for `RUN` steps, similar to how `/dev/null` is already handled. This would be a small, surgical change that fixes TLS for all users of chroot isolation with modern base images.

## Why Not Use OCI Isolation?

The primary use case for `--isolation=chroot` is Kubernetes, where OCI isolation fails because `/sys/fs/cgroup` is read-only. This is also the [recommended approach from buildah maintainers](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1481). So chroot isolation needs to work with modern images.

## Related Issues

- #3833 â€” Podman build random number seed problem (fixed via `ptrace` in seccomp defaults, but that fix only applies to OCI isolation, not chroot)

comments.body: Closing to re-file using the issue template.
